body:
Using `$(Base.julia_cmd())` instead of `julia` in MPI tests is what's [recommended in MPI.jl documentation](https://juliaparallel.org/MPI.jl/stable/usage/#Writing-MPI-tests) and not doing so is problematic for multiple reasons:

* you end up calling a julia process with compiler flags different than the launching process, which in some cases can [cause precompilation failures](https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48039)
* furthermore, because of the point above, you now have a bunch of concurrent processes which are all trying to precompile the environment at the same time, stepping on each other's toes, [causing absolute chaos](https://github.com/CliMA/Oceananigans.jl/actions/runs/21729692149/job/62692071513?pr=5246#step:6:1005)

I'd even go further and use `--compiled-modules=strict` which would make a process terminate with an error if a module isn't precompiled (that's what I ***strongly*** recommend doing on clusters, you really don't want wasting hours of compute time because of this).

labels.description: Tests get priority in case of emergency evacuation
labels.name: testing 🧪

labels.description: Our plan for total cluster domination
labels.name: distributed 🕸️

title: Always run `Base.julia_cmd()` in distributed tests

body:
I know some people are unhappy with the new whitespace check, and here is a way to address the situation: pre-commit is a mechanism to automatically resolve some issues (in this case, whitespace) upon `git commit`.  I added a section to the docs in the Contributing page to explain how to use it.

The pre-commit configuration is adapted from [`Pkg.jl`](https://github.com/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/blob/841395ebb16b23fde2ba9a010ccdce85f904abdf/.pre-commit-config.yaml).

This is basically same PR as https://github.com/NumericalEarth/Breeze.jl/pull/449.
title: Add pre-commit hook and explain how to use it

body: Extends `OceananigansReactantExt/` such that we can do planning with `ConcreteRArray`, enabling us to build `NonhydrostaticModel` on top of `ReactantState` with periodic bcs. Adds a test for such a build.
title: Support FFT planning for `ConcreteRArray`

body:
Other models that follow the same pattern (such as the `AtmosphereModel` in breeze) does not timestep the same exact variables (for example the atmosphere model updates `model.momentum` instead of `model.velocities`).

Therefore, this PR removes the explicit timestepping of `model.velocities` in favor of a more generic timestepping of `prognostic_fields(model)`
title: Do not explicitly step `model.velocities` for pressure_correction models
